title: podman ignores /etc/containers/storage.conf
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** Hello there, I am new to Podman and experiencing some problems getting it to work properly on a VPS. I think that podman somehow ignores my `/etc/containers/storage.conf`. Every command was executed by the user `root`. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Install `podman` ``` . /etc/os-release echo "deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list curl -L "https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key" | sudo apt-key add - apt update apt upgrade -y apt install podman -y ``` 2. Create container from image and run it ``` podman run --rm -it hello-world ``` - Describe the results you received: ``` ERRO[0002] error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/25d393690c911695de4e8de96324d830a824c1f514826d4be106bc7d764cf440/merged: invalid argument Error: error mounting storage for container 853344fd7594cbbd08220f5f87b1bed0dad8f249ba1d5e5c56217a88c8a755ca: error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/25d393690c911695de4e8de96324d830a824c1f514826d4be106bc7d764cf440/merged, mount_data="nodev,metacopy=on,lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/4H5GVDV2CA4XLRN66FU4K5UVKI,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/25d393690c911695de4e8de96324d830a824c1f514826d4be106bc7d764cf440/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/25d393690c911695de4e8de96324d830a824c1f514826d4be106bc7d764cf440/work": invalid argument ``` 3. Solution 1 ``` podman --storage-driver=overlay run --rm -it hello-world ``` - Describe the results you received: ``` Error: creating cgroup directory `/sys/fs/cgroup/net_prio,net_cls/machine.slice/libpod-e786693ce1ed18b65dbb7c146080f8669100df4e5a20f221cfb04006d7c03e38.scope`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found ``` 4. Solution 2 ``` podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=overlay run --rm -it hello-world ``` - Describe the results you received: ``` Everything is fine. ``` 5. Change content of file `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf` to: ``` ... [engine] ... cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs" ... ``` 6. Solution 3 ``` podman --storage-driver=overlay run --rm -it hello-world ``` - Describe the results you received: ``` Everything is fine. ``` 7. Change content of file `/etc/containers/storage.conf` to: ``` ... [storage] ... driver = "overlay" ... [storage.options.overlay] ... mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs" ... ``` 8. Solution 4 ``` podman run --rm -it hello-world ``` - Describe the results you received: ``` Error: error resolving storage path for container 0599d1423684c1ef13a26ce47e3b6e6fa5831077155eb17826b27ee32310387b: lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/fa2528fb70bdf6664f14a94ce22f05bb049871dad43d15747dd1f8057507fc1a/merged: invalid argument ``` - Describe the results you expected: ``` I expect the operation to complete without errors. ``` **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.4.2 API Version: 3.4.2 Go Version: go1.16.6 Built: Thu Jan 1 01:00:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.23.1 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - cpuacct - memory - devices - freezer - net_cls - blkio - perf_event - hugetlb - pids - net_prio cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon' path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.30, commit: ' cpus: 1 distribution: codename: focal distribution: ubuntu version: "20.04" eventLogger: journald hostname: h2925577.stratoserver.net idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 5.4.0 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 236621824 memTotal: 536870912 ociRuntime: name: crun package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun' path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.3.7-506ba commit: 7b4a7042370eea7fb00d3a4da34332b26f080acd spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns' version: |- slirp4netns version 1.1.8 commit: unknown libslirp: 4.3.1-git SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.1 swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 1h 0m 32.22s (Approximately 0.04 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io - quay.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mount_program: Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs' Version: |- fusermount3 version: 3.9.0 fuse-overlayfs: version 1.5 FUSE library version 3.9.0 using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31 overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 2 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.4.2 Built: 0 BuiltTime: Thu Jan 1 01:00:00 1970 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.16.6 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.4.2 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** No **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
System Information
Virtualization openvz
Operating System Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS
Kernel Linux 5.4.0
Architecture x86-64

comments.body:
## Update

When executing the following command, I also get a error message:

```
podman start --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=overlay <CONTAINER ID>
```

```
Error: unable to start container "53a4b39289c5fd2ef202a4bf6a8664fb6cec5af835ff7f6cea9e9f63fa814589": error mounting storage for container 53a4b39289c5fd2ef202a4bf6a8664fb6cec5af835ff7f6cea9e9f63fa814589: failed to canonicalise path for "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ad6d9ff6160670f013a57c45fcf8246fb422a7e6afe2f5428db87a5023346403/merged": lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ad6d9ff6160670f013a57c45fcf8246fb422a7e6afe2f5428db87a5023346403/merged: invalid argument
```

I would expect the storage changes might require a `podman system reset` after being made, otherwise we'll use the previous version cached in the DB.

Does making the cgroup settings in `/etc/containers/containers.conf` work?

Hello and thank you for your comment @mheon. I have executed the command `podman system reset`. However, the behavior is the same as before.

`podman --storage-driver=overlay run --rm -it xyz` works.
`podman run --rm -it xyz` DOES NOT work.
`podman start --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=overlay xyz` DOES NOT work.
openvz cannot run container, I tried `docker` and `containerd`, not work, podman too.
What is the issue with openvz?  Does it remove capabilities?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I got no response to issue, I am closing.  Reopen if you have more feedback.
